briefs.title: Council to vote on blocking BC Housing complex care project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a motion that would commit the City to not support, approve, or endorse a BC Housing supportive and complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street. Councillor Lee brought forward the motion, citing a high volume of resident emails and sustained community opposition to the proposed site.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10 Council meeting to have your say before the vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BC Housing Imperial Street complex care motion

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Healthcare

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing Supportive Housing Project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Council will consider a supplementary petition from Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch — a follow-up to an earlier petition — signed by 981 residents opposing a proposed BC Housing supportive and complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street in Burnaby. The petition asks Council to reject the project. No decision has been made yet; Council is being asked to receive the petition for information.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting to follow the discussion on this housing proposal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — supplementary petition on 5389 Imperial St housing considered

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan covering 2026 to 2030. If approved, the plan would set out how the city expects to raise and spend money each year — starting with roughly $873 million in revenue and $1.35 billion in total spending in 2026 — with property taxes making up the largest share of revenue at about 45%, and businesses carrying 37.1% of the property tax burden.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — approval will lock in Burnaby's tax and spending plan through 2030.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw — council adoption vote

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Update on Burnaby's Online Recreation Registration System Improvements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an information update on improvements made to WebReg, the city's online recreation registration system, since it launched in 2023. The update covers changes like rolling registration windows for swim and skate lessons, a new flexible monthly pass option, removal of annual pass holds, and better online self-service tools for residents booking programs at city facilities.
briefs.action_required: If you use Burnaby recreation facilities, check the city website for updated pass options and registration rules.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 10 Park Development Projects for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive a report outlining 10 planned park improvement projects for 2026–2027, funded through the city's 2026–2030 capital plan. The projects include playground replacements, a new basketball court, spray park design, artificial turf replacement, trail management, and signage upgrades at parks across Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby's project webpage and social media for feedback surveys on parks near your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — 2026 Park Development Projects report considered

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan Moves to Drafting Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an update on the Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) Plan, which is now moving into Phase 3 — the drafting stage. Staff will use community feedback gathered in 2025 to write a draft plan covering 15 service areas, including parks, pools, arenas, and cultural facilities. A second round of public engagement is planned for March 2026, and the completed plan is expected later that spring.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 2026 public engagement — you can give feedback on the draft PRC Plan that will shape Burnaby's parks and recreation priorities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby PRC Plan Phase 4 public engagement opens — comment period

briefs.title: Council to Receive Burnaby Building Permit Activity Report for December 2025
briefs.summary: Council will consider receiving a routine information report on building permit activity in Burnaby for December 2025. The report shows 132 permits were issued that month, representing $152.9 million in construction value, and that 2025 saw a total of 1,225 permits worth over $3.15 billion — up significantly from prior years.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $10,000 for staff to attend 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on spending up to $10,000 to send up to six members of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee to the 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver, running April 28 – May 1, 2026. The BCRPA (British Columbia Recreation and Parks Association) is a non-profit group that supports parks and recreation across the province, and this is their main annual conference.

key_dates.label: 2026 BCRPA Symposium — Vancouver event dates

briefs.title: Council to vote on $380,000 contract renewal for road marking paint supplier
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with Cloverdale Paint Inc. to supply road marking paint and glass beads — the materials used to keep lane lines and pavement markings visible across city roads. The proposed two-year renewal (March 2026 to February 2028) would cost an estimated $380,000 including taxes, with no price increase per unit compared to the previous term.
briefs.action_required: No action required — this contract renewal does not directly affect most small businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby road marking paint contract renewal — Council vote
Cloverdale Paint contract renewal period begins — Burnaby

briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing Burnaby's temporary fence rental contract for $267,000
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its temporary fence rental contract with Super Save Fence Rentals Inc. for one final year (June 1, 2026 to May 31, 2027) at an estimated cost of $267,000 including taxes. The fencing supports city events, construction sites, and public safety needs — and the supplier has agreed to hold prices steady for this final term.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business supplies fencing or event services to the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fence rental contract renewal term starts — if approved
Burnaby fence rental contract renewal term ends

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $7.3M renewal of Burnaby's police dispatch contract with E-COMM
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its police dispatch services contract with E-COMM Emergency Communications for BC Incorporated for one year (January 1 to December 31, 2026) at an estimated cost of $7,300,000. The renewal — a 1% increase over last year — would keep the same contract terms in place while the City continues reviewing whether E-COMM's service levels meet Burnaby's needs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects Burnaby's public safety services budget.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — E-COMM police dispatch contract renewal 2026

briefs.title: Council to vote on $240,000 contract increase for hazardous building material surveys
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on adding $240,000 (including tax) to an existing contract with Astech Consultants Ltd. for pre-demolition hazardous building material surveys — inspections required before tearing down buildings to check for dangerous materials like asbestos. The extra funding is needed because the number of city-owned houses slated for demolition has grown, requiring more surveys than originally planned.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business does demolition or environmental consulting work for the City.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — contract change vote date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $193,000 contract change for seniors' housekeeping program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a $193,000 contract change (an increase to an existing agreement) with Comfort Keepers to keep delivering light housekeeping services to seniors under the Better at Home Program through December 31, 2026. The program is funded by a BC government grant through United Way and helps seniors live independently at home — costs are fully covered by that funding and fees paid by participating seniors.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you provide home support or senior care services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Better at Home housekeeping contract change — service end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $700,000 contract extension for archaeological consulting services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a $700,000 contract change — meaning an increase to an existing contract — with Campfire Archaeology and Heritage Ltd. If approved, the firm would continue providing archaeological consulting services to support City construction projects, help Burnaby meet its legal obligations under BC's Heritage Conservation Act, and advance the City's commitments to Truth and Reconciliation with Indigenous Peoples.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects firms working on City construction projects.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — archaeological consulting contract change approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on buying 3 more Toyota Tacoma trucks for $174,384
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on adding three Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks to an existing contract with OpenRoad Toyota Richmond, at an estimated cost of $174,384 including taxes. The trucks would replace three aging city vehicles, which would then be sold at auction. No new bidding process is needed — the original contract already allows the city to add units within 18 months.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $20M fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a five-year contract worth up to $20 million (including taxes) to 4Refuel Canada LP for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, biodiesel, and related fuel products to City facilities. The contract would run from March 11, 2026 to March 10, 2031, with the option to extend for two additional one-year periods if service and pricing remain satisfactory.
briefs.action_required: No action required for most businesses — this is an internal City supply contract.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP — proposed start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $648,206 contract for new Burnaby garbage truck
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $648,206 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the purchase of a new tandem axle front load refuse truck — a large commercial garbage truck used for curbside collection. The new truck would replace an older unit that will be sold at auction once the replacement arrives.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects waste collection services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — refuse truck contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $621,838 contract for two new split body refuse trucks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $621,838 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the purchase of two Ford/HaulAll split body refuse trucks. These trucks would replace two aging city vehicles, which would then be sold at auction. The funding would come from the city's Equipment Replacement Reserve Fund.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Permanently Closing Lochdale School Park Junior Playground
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on whether to permanently decommission the junior playground at Lochdale School Park and delay its replacement until future budget cycles can fund it. The playground was closed in December 2025 after it became unsafe and no longer met national safety standards. Staff recommend deferring any replacement rather than pulling money from other planned park projects.
briefs.action_required: If you use or rely on this park, attend the February 10 council meeting to share your views before the vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Lochdale School Park playground decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw update for advisory body oversight
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on amendments to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 that would change how the rules (called 'Terms of Reference') for the City's advisory bodies are created and updated. Under the proposed changes, the Mayor would direct the preparation of these rules, and a review committee would check them before they go to Council for final approval. Public notice is required before the bylaw can be adopted.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public notice posting — you can review the proposed changes on the City's website under Public Notices.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 amendment — Council consideration meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on removing restaurant-only restriction at Burnaby industrial property
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on removing a legal restriction (called a covenant) that currently requires a 2,217 sq. ft. space at 8340 Fraser Reach Court to be used only as a restaurant. The property owner, Oxford Properties, has tried for years to find a restaurant tenant with no success, so staff are recommending the restriction be lifted so the space can be used for light industrial purposes instead.
briefs.action_required: If you are interested in leasing this space as a restaurant, contact Oxford Properties before the Feb 10 vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — covenant discharge at 8340 Fraser Reach Court

briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on five new non-residential zoning districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts — Employment (E1), Industrial (I1), Parks & Natural Areas (PM1), Institutional (PM2), and Agriculture (AM1) — to put the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (the city's long-term land use blueprint) into effect. If approved, the new rules would change what types of businesses and uses are allowed on affected properties, including new conditions on storage facilities, industrial uses, and housing near SkyTrain stations.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls under a new district — the new rules could change what uses are permitted on your lot.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw new non-residential districts — Council vote meeting date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Agriculture

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Update on Second Narrows Dredging Works
briefs.summary: The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is set to appear before Burnaby City Council on February 10, 2026, to give an update on dredging works at Second Narrows. Dredging means removing sediment from the bottom of a waterway to keep it deep enough for ships. Representatives will present slides and answer questions from Council and the community.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting if the Second Narrows dredging works affect your business.

key_dates.label: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Second Narrows dredging update — Burnaby Council presentation

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Approves 2026–2030 Financial Plan and Several Major Contracts
briefs.summary: At its January 27, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of items including a five-year financial plan, housing agreement changes, and several construction and service contracts totalling millions of dollars. These decisions cover city spending, infrastructure upgrades, and housing policy that could affect businesses operating in or doing work with the City of Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Review the 2026–2030 Financial Plan to understand how city spending may affect your business or contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Consolidated Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw 2026 — third reading passed

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite to Reshape Rules for Employment, Industrial & Agricultural Land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a major rewrite of its Zoning Bylaw — the rulebook that controls what businesses can operate where. The proposed changes would cut the number of zoning categories from 53 down to 15, add new restrictions on self-storage facilities near SkyTrain stations, update rules for farms and industrial land, and allow limited housing near transit on employment-zoned land.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed zoning changes at burnaby.ca to see if your business location or land use could be affected.
